steps.title: Use the correct graph shape
steps.content: Here $f(x)=2^x$, which is increasing and curves upward. The line $g$ is also increasing and intersects $f$ at two x-values, $h$ and $k$, with $h\lt k$.

steps.title: Use a sample Desmos model
steps.content: A representative example is $y=2^x$ and the increasing line $y=2x$. They intersect at $x=1$ and $x=2$. Between those intersections, the line is above the exponential curve; outside them, the exponential curve is above the line.

steps.title: Apply that to h and k
steps.content:
Since $g(x)\gt f(x)$ means the line is above the exponential graph, this happens between the two intersection x-values:
$$h\lt x\lt k$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The required condition is $h\lt x\lt k$, which is choice D. So D is correct.
